skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
test_name: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 2
passage: A rectangle has a length that is 49 times its width. The function $y = (49w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $49w$ in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: B
choices.text: The area of the rectangle, in square feet

choices.label: C
choices.text: The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: D
choices.text: The width of the rectangle, in feet
